entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: The most important element required by all living organisms for the synthesis of complex organic molecules

entities.description: Complex organic molecules synthesized using nitrogen

entities.name: NITROGEN CYCLE
entities.description: A complex biogeochemical cycle involving oxic and anoxic reactions for the recycling of essential chemical requirements

entities.name: CARBON CYCLE
entities.description: A major biogeochemical cycle compared to the nitrogen cycle

entities.name: ORGANIC N MINERALIZATION
entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle

entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle

entities.name: NITRIFICATION
entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle

entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle

entities.name: ANAEROBIC AMMONIUM OXIDATION
entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle also known as anammox

entities.name: DISSIMILATORY NITRATE REDUCTION TO AMMONIUM
entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle also known as DNRA

entities.name: COMAMMOX
entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle

entities.name: CODENITRIFICATION
entities.description: An oxic or anoxic reaction within the nitrogen cycle

entities.name: SOIL MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms that improve nitrogen cycle balance and support sustainable agricultural practices

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural methods paved by soil microorganisms to improve soil and crop productivity

entities.name: SOIL PROPERTIES
entities.description: Attributes of soil improved by the activity of soil microorganisms

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop yield improved by soil microorganisms and nitrogen availability

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Organisms that uptake soluble nitrogen and influence microbial nitrogen transformations

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Factors that can result in a shift in the equilibrium state of nitrogen-cycling processes

entities.description: Environments where microorganisms operate the nitrogen cycle
